rulings.Case Title: JASON SELLO VS. COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 6, 2023 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT SOTHEBY'S INTERNATIONAL REALTY, INC., MARY LOU CASTELLANOS DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to July 7, 2023 per stipulation and order. =(501/SLH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 1440 SUTTER STREET, LLC VS. STEFANIE RISTICK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 6, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT STEFANIE RISTICK Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Judgment is DENIED. Defendant makes this motion based on purported inability to oppose the motion for summary judgment. Defendant's opposition to the Motion for Summary Judgment, while ultimately rejected for filing, was considered by the Court in making its ruling on Plaintiff's motion for Summary Judgment as evidenced by the Minutes (Court sustained Plaintiff's objections to Defendant's evidence) and the Court Order (listing Defendant's response to the Separate Statement). =(501/SLH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
